title: espeletia episcopalis
intro: Espeletia episcopalis, also known as 'the queen of the Andes', is a plant native to South America, specifically in the high-altitude regions of Colombia and Ecuador. It belongs to the daisy family and is prized for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, offering several health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. In herbal preparations, Espeletia episcopalis is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be guided by a healthcare professional due to potential interactions with other medications.
